skill.name: zendesk-automation
skill.description: Automate Zendesk ticket, user, and organization workflows through Rube MCP and Composio Zendesk tools.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants through Zendesk automation using Rube MCP tool discovery and safe workflow sequencing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown backticks, tool names, and parameter examples rather than executable Ruby, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance code. The confirmed concern is legitimate but material: the skill uses an external Rube MCP endpoint and guides agents through Zendesk operations, including destructive ticket deletion.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Service Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is expected for the workflow, but it means Zendesk automation depends on an external service that can broker actions against connected Zendesk accounts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint is explicitly documented on line 20. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because the skill describes it as the required MCP service for Zendesk automation.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Zendesk Ticket Operation
medium_findings.description: The skill includes a Zendesk ticket deletion workflow and states that deletion is permanent. This is a legitimate administrative capability, but accidental or unauthorized use could cause irreversible support data loss.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The delete tool and permanence warnings are present in multiple locations. This confirms operational risk, although the text does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are inline Markdown code spans, fenced examples, or Zendesk tool names. No executable Ruby file, shell command, or user-controlled command execution path was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the cited syntax is used for formatting tool names, parameters, and workflow examples. No script file or executable command wrapper is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in natural language, YAML metadata, tool names, table text, and parameter names. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata and a quick-reference table, not cryptographic code. The scanner appears to have matched substrings inside ordinary Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines are Zendesk workflow instructions for finding users, reading ticket or organization IDs, and using pagination. No host, network, or system enumeration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe Zendesk object lookup fields and tool slugs. They do not instruct OS or network reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permanent Deletion Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a delete-ticket tool and warns that deleted tickets cannot be recovered. This capability should require explicit user confirmation and authorization checks before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete capability and irreversibility warning are directly documented. The concern is misuse or accidental execution, not hidden malicious code.

content.value_statement: Support teams lose time repeating ticket, user, and organization tasks across Zendesk. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for using Rube MCP Zendesk tools.
actual_capabilities: Search, list, retrieve, create, update, reply to, and delete Zendesk tickets through Rube MCP tools.
Find and create Zendesk users, including requester and assignee records.
List, retrieve, create, update, and count Zendesk organizations.
Guide agents to search current Rube tool schemas before calling Zendesk tools.
Document pagination, safe update, tag replacement, and rate limit handling for Zendesk workflows.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and an authenticated Zendesk connection.
Does not include executable code or direct Zendesk API calls outside MCP tools.
Tool schemas may change, so the assistant must search tools before each workflow.
Destructive actions such as ticket deletion require separate user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Triage Support Queues
use_cases.description: Review open or urgent tickets, inspect details, and prepare updates using current Zendesk tool schemas.

use_cases.title: Maintain Customer Records
use_cases.description: Search users, create missing requesters, and connect users with organizations before ticket work.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Agent Replies
use_cases.description: Draft public replies or internal notes after checking the current ticket state and visibility settings.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Tickets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Zendesk automation skill to search current Rube tool schemas, then list the first page of recent Zendesk tickets sorted by updated time.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Customer Ticket
prompt_templates.prompt: Find or create the requester in Zendesk, then create a normal priority ticket with this subject and description. Show me the fields before submitting.

prompt_templates.title: Reply With Internal Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current Zendesk ticket state, draft an internal note, and set public to false. Ask for confirmation before posting.

prompt_templates.title: Update With Conflict Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the ticket, preserve existing tags, add the requested tag, and update the status using safe_update with the current updated_stamp.

output_examples.input: List high priority tickets updated most recently.
output_examples.output: A concise ticket list with ticket IDs, subjects, statuses, priorities, requester details, and the next page status.

output_examples.input: Create a ticket for a customer who is not in Zendesk.
output_examples.output: A requester search summary, proposed user creation details, ticket fields, and a confirmation step before creating records.

output_examples.input: Add an internal note to ticket 12345.
output_examples.output: A ticket state summary, the proposed private note text, and a confirmation request before posting the note.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before each workflow because available parameters can change.
Fetch current ticket or organization data before updates to avoid replacing tags or stale fields.
Require explicit confirmation for public replies, user creation, organization changes, and deletion.
anti_patterns: Do not delete tickets without a clear approval and recovery review.
Do not assume replies are private; set public to false for internal notes.
Do not overwrite tags without merging them with the current record.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Zendesk?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant to use Zendesk tools exposed through Rube MCP and Composio.

faq.question: Do I need an authenticated Zendesk connection?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires an active Zendesk connection through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS before workflows can run.

faq.question: Can it create and update tickets?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for creating tickets, updating fields, adding replies, and managing status.

faq.question: Can it manage users and organizations?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers user search and creation, plus organization listing, retrieval, creation, updates, and counts.

faq.question: Why must the assistant search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current names, parameters, and constraints.

faq.question: Is ticket deletion supported?
faq.answer: Yes, but deletion is permanent. The assistant should request explicit confirmation before using delete workflows.
